grades.expectation: Response mentions s84 Law of Property Act 1925 as an option for modification/discharge
grades.reason: Explicitly mentions Section 84 of the Law of Property Act 1925 for discharge/modification via Upper Tribunal

grades.expectation: Response mentions restrictive covenant indemnity insurance with approximate cost range
grades.reason: Provides detailed cost table with ranges from £150-£800+ based on property value

grades.expectation: Response states that approaching the covenant beneficiary invalidates indemnity insurance
grades.reason: Clearly states the 'Golden Rule' that contacting beneficiaries will close the insurance market and make policies unobtainable

grades.expectation: Response assesses risk factors: age of covenant (60yr+), no enforcement history, minimal breach
grades.reason: Notes covenant is from 1965 (60+ years), mentions no enforcement history, and discusses de minimis breach for quiet home office

grades.expectation: Response mentions Upper Tribunal as the forum for s84 applications
grades.reason: Specifically identifies Upper Tribunal (Lands Chamber) as the forum for Section 84 applications
